zh.Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Johanna Joan de Massey於1375年出生在英國爾塔頓時，她的父親Lord John Massey31歲，母親Alice Worsley27歲。她於1391年11月8日與William Richard de Venables of Bollen在英國英格蘭爾伯頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1420年在英國爾享年45歲，埋葬在英國爾。

zh-hans.Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England: 英国英格兰史洛普爾史福斯波利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Johanna Joan de Massey于1375年出生在英国爾塔頓时，她的父亲Lord John Massey31岁，母亲Alice Worsley27岁。她于1391年11月8日与William Richard de Venables of Bollen在英国英格兰爾伯頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1420年在英国爾享年45岁，埋葬在英国爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Masey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: Johanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of Johannes ( see John ).

variants.name: Johannah
meanings: feminine form of Johannes ( see John ).

variants.name: Jonna
meanings: contracted form of   Johanna .

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Johanna Joan de Massey was born in 1375, in Tatton, Cheshire, England, her father, Lord John Massey, was 31 and her mother, Alice Worsley, was 27. She married William Richard de Venables of Bollen on 8 November 1391, in Burton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1420, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 45, and was buried in Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Johanna Joan de Massey est née en 1375, à Tatton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Lord John Massey, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Alice Worsley, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé William Richard de Venables of Bollen le 8 novembre 1391, à Burton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1420, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterrée à Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Johanna Joan de Massey 1375 in Tatton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lord John Massey 31 und ihre Mutter Alice Worsley 27. Sie hat am 8. November 1391 in Burton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom William Richard de Venables of Bollen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1420 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Cheshire, England und wurde in Cheshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Johanna Joan de Massey è nata il nel 1375 nel Tatton, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Lord John Massey, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Alice Worsley, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato William Richard de Venables of Bollen il 8 novembre 1391, nel Burton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1420, nel Cheshire, England, all'età di 45 anni, e fu sepolta a Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Johanna Joan de Masseyが1375年にイギリスチェシュアイアタットオンで生まれた時，父Lord John Masseyは31歳，母Alice Worsleyは27歳であった。1391年11月8日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアブルトンでWilliam Richard de Venables of Bollenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1420年にイギリスチェシュアイアで45歳で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Johanna Joan de Massey(이)가 1375년 영국 체실르 타턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord John Massey(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Worsley(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1391년 11월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 버턴에서 William Richard de Venables of Bollen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1420년 영국 체실르에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Johanna Joan de Massey nasceu em 1375, em Tatton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lord John Massey, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Alice Worsley, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com William Richard de Venables of Bollen em 8 de novembro de 1391, em Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1420, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 45 anos, e foi sepultada em Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Johanna Joan de Massey родилась в 1375 г. в местности таттон, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Lord John Massey было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Alice Worsley, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Richard de Venables of Bollen 8 ноября 1391 г. в местности буртон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1420 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 45 лет и была похоронена в местности чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Johanna Joan de Massey nació en 1375, en Tatton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Lord John Massey, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Alice Worsley, tenía 27 años. Se casó con William Richard de Venables of Bollen el 8 de noviembre de 1391, en Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1420, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 45 años, y fue sepultada en Cheshire, Inglaterra.
